title: podman exec: Error: retrieving exec session exit code: exec died ... unable to find event
body:
```
[+1565s] not ok 309 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
...
 in test file test/system/[260-sdnotify.bats, line 334](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/359e397443093f038ae7a8c4d0d0e5f07b5c6506/test/system/260-sdnotify.bats#L334))
...
# podman exec test_pod-a /bin/touch /stop
Error: retrieving exec session f76f94e20cca67d445ce47e6b76c31318487b25011105d333490cd3719ea9f00 exit code:
       exec died event for session f76f94e20cca67d445ce47e6b76c31318487b25011105d333490cd3719ea9f00
       (container b928cbf38b3ff606dbf088243c943c3f64a5a8ec416c57674c25c64e64244587) not found:
       unable to find event
[ rc=255 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
First seen Sep 27. So far, `aarch64` only.

### [sys] 309 sdnotify : play kube - with policies
* fedora-36-aarch64 : sys podman fedora-36-aarch64 root host
  * PR #15987
    * [09-29 10:15](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5646507218042880/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00309)
  * PR #15962
    * [09-27 13:00](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5177041942216704/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00309)
  * PR #15782
    * [09-29 14:28](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5501772071436288/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00309)

comments.body:
New one today on v4.3

* fedora-36-aarch64 : sys podman fedora-36-aarch64 root host
  * PR #16241
    * [10-20 14:30](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5673483152654336/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00312)

Seen right now, in one of my 1minutetip VMs (f36):
```

   956b6f00f6ed  localhost/podman-pause:4.3.0-dev-1666791953              1 second ago            Up Less than a second ago              70db9b932c76-infra

af0747f0e091c7b3aa83c043f06e6dc3316c7c6552 (container 23d5e5aefc3837f786ea74a6fe307071be994d92e786dd847a7554f3b32e8bfe) not found: unable to find event

 ```
Sorry for the wall of text; it seems important since there's no linkable log.

Oh, and, this is important: my VM is `x86_64`, so this is no longer an aarch64-only bug.
This one looks interesting, I'll take a look.
Ran out of time.  It's bookmarked, so I won't forget but if others are interested, please tackle it.

Here's one in CI, [f36 root](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6661945649528832/html/sys-podman-fedora-36-root-host.log.html#t--00312). The interesting thing (to me) is that it's triggering in the "play kube - with policies" test, and that's the same test associated with #16076. Could be coincidence. Or there could be a connection worth exploring.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Instances in past month:

* fedora-37 : sys podman fedora-37 root host
  * PR #16517
    * [11-17 07:57](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5135680568295424/html/sys-podman-fedora-37-root-host.log.html#t--00317)
  * PR #16389
    * [11-02-2022 19:28](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4933082733084672/html/sys-podman-fedora-37-root-host.log.html#t--00313)
* fedora-37-aarch64 : sys podman fedora-37-aarch64 root host
  * PR #16682
    * [12-03 08:23](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5528694907731968/html/sys-podman-fedora-37-aarch64-root-host.log.html#t--00321)
Haven't seen this since the 12-03 instance noted above.

Although it doesn't feel like an achievement, I will close it for now.  The tests (and code) was changed quite a bit in December, so we may got rid of the flake.

If it ever comes back, let's reopen.
